Patch Set 1:

It appears that teh test failure is related to this patch.

See OSS logs: https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_logs/9c2925ea-4da0-11e5-862f-5254006e85c2/show_text

ptlrpc_send_new_req()) ASSERTION( req->rq_xid == 0 ) failed

so somehow xid is not 0, which if it's a resend is totally plausible

Patch Set 1:

My bad, I conducted my own tests on a Lustre version that was lacking the patch from http://review.whamcloud.com/14793 , which added the assertion.

I think the assertion can be replaced with a simple test, and we can assign a new xid in case the request does not have one yet.

Patch Set 2:

Right, the previous patch (http://review.whamcloud.com/15070) breaks current multi-rcvd slot too.

The situation will be different after http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/15473/ landed. That patch changes the way of getting min unreplied XID and doesn't rely on the sending/delay list anymore.

Patch Set 2:

OK so, if I understand correctly, we should wait for http://review.whamcloud.com/15473 to land before refreshing this patch.


And without http://review.whamcloud.com/15473, the multi-rcvd slot feature is broken in the case the enc_pool runs out of free pages when using krb5p Kerberos feature.

commit.subject: LU-7051 ptlrpc: remove code duplication in ptlrpc_check_set
commit.message:
LU-7051 ptlrpc: remove code duplication in ptlrpc_check_set

In ptlrpc_check_set(), instead of calling ptl_send_rpc() explicitly
in case rpc needs to be resent, just remove request from the list, put
request back in NEW state, and continue.
This requires to remove LASSERT(req->rq_xid == 0) in
ptlrpc_send_new_req(), because having rq_xid not 0 in case of resend
is totally plausible.

Also remove an erroneous comment about rq_wait_ctx and ptlrpcd
threads.

Signed-off-by: Sebastien Buisson <sebastien.buisson@bull.net>
Signed-off-by: Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>
Change-Id: I498815f692b7af99501715c0e2fab67a661899d5
